message:
fix(transport): assert non-zero channel capacity to prevent silent deadlock

SharedChannel::new previously accepted capacity=0 without complaint, which
would create a channel that could never buffer a message — any send would
block forever waiting for space that can never exist. This is a logic
error at the call site, not a deliberate rendezvous pattern.

Add a panic assertion in SharedChannel::new and document the panics
condition on the public `channel()` factory function.

Changes:
- src/transport/mod.rs: assert!(capacity > 0) in SharedChannel::new,
  #[doc] panics section on channel(), new test
  test_channel_zero_capacity_panics using #[should_panic]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
